idea-plugin.name: Sightline
idea-plugin.description:
Claude Code inside Android Studio, with visible project activity and controlled tool execution.

Sightline provides a native Android Studio interface for a locally installed Claude Code CLI.

Use it to:

  • Chat with Claude without leaving Android Studio.
  • Attach files and IDE context, and remove any of it before you send.
  • Review tool requests and proposed changes as diffs before they apply.
  • See files, commands, tests and relationships in the Activity Map.
  • Inspect structured build, test and diagnostic results.

Android-aware. Sightline knows your build variant, connected device, module and running process, so you don't have to explain them. It reads Gradle failures into typed diagnoses, resolves crashes back to the line that threw, picks the tests worth running for what you changed, and audits your merged manifest. Logcat is redacted before it can reach a prompt.

Requires the Claude Code CLI (the claude command), installed and authenticated separately. Sightline does not provide Claude access, does not handle your credentials, and has no login screen or backend of its own.

No telemetry. No analytics, no usage statistics, nothing sent anywhere except to the local claude process you started. Your conversation is never written to disk.

Sightline is an independent project and is not affiliated with or endorsed by Anthropic, JetBrains or Google. "Claude" and "Claude Code" are trademarks of Anthropic; "Android Studio" is a trademark of Google. Both are used only to describe compatibility.

Copyright © 2026 Michael Carroll. Source-available — the source is published so it can be read and audited, but it is not open source and may not be redistributed. Using Sightline in your work, including commercially, is expressly permitted. Free during the beta. See the licence accompanying this listing.
idea-plugin.change-notes:
0.1.0-beta — first public release.

  • Chat panel — streaming replies, extended thinking, Markdown with syntax-highlighted code, collapsible tool cards, and edit diffs you review before they apply.
  • Permission control — five modes, inline Allow / Allow-always / Deny, a path guard that refuses credential locations outright, and a device gate that always confirms anything destroying app data.
  • Agent Activity Map — a live graph of what Claude is observably touching, with the evidence for every relationship it draws.
  • Android control centre — build-variant and device context supplied automatically, typed Gradle failure diagnosis, crash-to-source resolution, targeted test selection, redacted logcat, screen inspection, Compose and manifest analysis.
  • No telemetry, and no conversation persistence.

This is a beta. Please report problems on the public issue tracker.
